title: Galathea National Park
tagline: Galathea National Park — wildlife reserve of Andaman & Nicobar

overview.short: Galathea National Park is a national park located in the Union Territory of Andaman and Nicobar Islands, India.
overview.description: Galathea National Park is a national park located in the Union Territory of Andaman and Nicobar Islands, India. It is located on the island of Great Nicobar in the Nicobar Islands, which lie in the eastern Indian Ocean (Bay of Bengal). Many unique and rare species of plants and animals are found in the park, several of which (owing to their relative geographical isolation) are endemic to the islands.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Galathea National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Galathea National Park is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Galathea National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Veer Savarkar International Airport, Port Blair (538 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (1634 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Galathea National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Galathea National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Galathea National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Galathea National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Galathea National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kokeon, Joginder Nagar, Great Nicobar, Laxmi Nagar, Great Nicobar.
